paragraphs.text:
# Stability Constants for the Cu(II)–Glycine System

**Source:** NIST Standard Reference Database 46 (SRD-46)

The Cu²⁺–glycine system (ligand defined as HL = aminoacetic acid) has **two reported cumulative formation equilibria** in SRD-46:

| Equilibrium | Definition |
|---|---|
| **β₁** (CuL) | Cu²⁺ + L⁻ ⇌ CuL |
| **β₂** (CuL₂) | Cu²⁺ + 2 L⁻ ⇌ CuL₂ |

---

paragraphs.text:
## Thermodynamic Parameters (at 25 °C unless noted)

| Species | Parameter | Value | T (°C) | I (mol/L) |
|---------|-----------|-------|---------|-----------|
| CuL | ΔH | −26.8 kJ/mol | 10 | 0 |
| CuL | ΔH | −25.1 kJ/mol | 25 | 0 |
| CuL | ΔH | −25.1 kJ/mol | 25 | 0.1 |
| CuL | ΔH | −23.4 kJ/mol | 40 | 0 |
| CuL | ΔS | 79.5 J/(mol·K) | 25 | 0 |
| CuL | ΔS | 72.4 J/(mol·K) | 25 | 0.1 |
| CuL₂ | ΔH | −56.5 kJ/mol | 10 | 0 |
| CuL₂ | ΔH | −54.4 kJ/mol | 25 | 0 |
| CuL₂ | ΔH | −54.4 kJ/mol | 25 | 0.1 |
| CuL₂ | ΔH | −52.3 kJ/mol | 40 | 0 |
| CuL₂ | ΔS | 118.0 J/(mol·K) | 25 | 0 |
| CuL₂ | ΔS | 106.7 J/(mol·K) | 25 | 0.1 |

paragraphs.text:
## Chemistry Insights

The **recommended values at 25 °C, I = 0** are log β₁ = **8.56** and log β₂ = **15.70**, making the stepwise log K₂ ≈ 7.14 — only slightly lower than log K₁, which is characteristic of Cu(II) amino acid complexes where the second glycinate binds in a *trans* square-planar arrangement with minimal steric penalty. The strongly negative ΔH values confirm that complex formation is enthalpy-driven (ligand-field stabilization of the d⁹ Cu²⁺ ion), while the positive ΔS contributions reflect desolvation of both the metal aqua ion and the glycinate anion upon chelation. The modest decrease in log K with increasing ionic strength (at 25 °C) follows the expected Debye–Hückel trend for reactions that reduce the net charge of the species in solution.

ligand-field stabilization of the d⁹ Cu²⁺ ion
desolvation of both the metal aqua ion and the glycinate anion upon chelation
modest decrease in log K with increasing ionic strength
reduce the net charge of the species in solution
